mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS anchored near zero (0.12%), the persistent near-zero policy rate keeps the equity risk premium compressed relative to bonds, generating a structural tailwind for S&P 500 returns over the following month as investors continue to reach for yield in risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-04, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses below 5.5% while still elevated relative to pre-crisis norms, it signals labor market tightening that raises expectations of future Fed tightening, producing a negative drift in S&P 500 returns over the next month as discount rate expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.5% for two consecutive monthly readings
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% as of 2015-04, below 5.5% threshold
regime_hint: labor market tightening phase ahead of policy normalization
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following UNRATE crossing below 5.5% are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deceleration in PAYEMS growth and INDPRO growth over a quarter signals a synchronized slowdown in both labor demand and industrial output that historically precedes a broader earnings growth slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. Neither series alone captures this because divergence between labor and production cycles is common in mid-cycle soft patches.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS 141367K and INDPRO 105.18 as of early 2015
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with growth deceleration risk
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-INDPRO deceleration signal shows Rank IC below 0.03 versus 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond either series alone (incremental R^2 < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime, rising HOUST housing starts reflect strengthening rate-sensitive demand that supports broader consumer wealth effects and construction-linked earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This mechanism is regime-specific: in a tightening-rate regime, rising starts instead signal overheating and precede negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 926K units and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015
regime_hint: zero-interest-rate policy regime with accommodative monetary stance
failure_condition: Positive HOUST-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03 or sign flips) when tested specifically within periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS retail sales reflects robust consumer spending momentum that translates into near-term positive earnings surprises, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 436772 million dollars as of 2015-04
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO growth decelerates below zero on a year-over-year basis, it signals an emerging industrial contraction that raises recession risk pricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as equity risk premia widen.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following negative INDPRO YoY growth are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances historically, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The joint state of near-zero FEDFUNDS combined with elevated UNRATE above 5% indicates substantial labor market slack that justifies continued monetary accommodation, and this combination produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter because it anchors low-rate expectations and delays tightening risk. Neither series alone is sufficient since a low rate with low unemployment would instead signal imminent tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 0.12% and UNRATE 5.4% as of early 2015
regime_hint: monetary accommodation amid labor market slack
failure_condition: Interaction of low FEDFUNDS and elevated UNRATE shows Rank IC below 0.03 against 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or incremental predictive power over either series alone is negligible (delta R^2 < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month growth in PAYEMS nonfarm payrolls signals ongoing labor market expansion and income growth that supports near-term risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141367 thousand persons as of 2015-04
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 5-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
